title: Financial and legal MVP1 acceptance plan defined
objective: Define the connected MVP1 acceptance proof in which one governed financial workflow and one governed legal workflow both run intent-to-verified-next-action through the SAME LifeOS substrate — the governed execution spine (LIFEOS-003: identity/authority, policy, task graph, memory, simulation, human-approval, hermetic execution, proof, rollback, next-action) sitting on the intelligence substrate (LIFEOS-005: hot/warm/cold memory, proof-derived learning, secrets brokerage, promotion of proven state only) — with store ownership fixed by STORE-001. Acceptance is proven when both workflows, driven by real-shaped sanitized fixtures, are covered for every gate clause: real inputs, authority, simulation, execution-or-deliberate-non-action, proof, rollback, next action, and measurable user value. This artifact DEFINES the acceptance contract; it performs no financial or legal action and mutates no live state.
supporting_sources: 1.0_VISION/Notebooklm/Architecture Context Matrix_ PostgreSQL-First LifeOS Framework - Table 1.csv

generated/task_graph.source.csv rows MVP1-001 (financial slice), MVP1-002 (legal slice), LIFEOS-003 (governed execution spine), LIFEOS-005 (intelligence substrate), LIFEOS-006 (financial+legal operating domain)
store_authority_reference: STORE-001 — PostgreSQL + RuVector is the single canonical durable owner of relational + semantic macro-state; redb is the transient high-frequency tier (intent/thoughts/traces/WAL/dedup); AgentDB (.rvf) is per-agent non-authoritative cognition; envctl is the forward-only bridge and projection engine; OS keyring holds external secrets. No durable class is silently reclassified; forward-only redb -> envctl(embed) -> PostgreSQL; single durable writer is PostgreSQL.
verification_gate: Acceptance covers real inputs with sanitized fixtures, authority, simulation, execution or deliberate non-action, proof, rollback, next action, and measurable user value.
execution_gate_note: This artifact DEFINES the acceptance criteria for the connected financial+legal MVP1 proof. It reads no live bank/legal account, sends no notice, submits no transfer, signs nothing, and writes no production row. Live execution of the two workflows is deferred to the proof-cell execution gate (execution_cell=proof-cell) after MVP1-001 and MVP1-002 fully bound their slices; per allowed/blocked paths, financial or legal action, production deployment, product implementation, and secrets/** are out of scope here.
hard_invariants: Observation precedes action: both workflows begin with read-only aggregation/understanding before any recommendation or cell write (MVP1-001, MVP1-002 next_action).
No unlimited agent authority: every write is scoped by an identity+policy authority contract; the agent may recommend but never self-authorizes a spend, trade, or legal commitment (LIFEOS-003 goal).

No autonomous spend or trading: the financial workflow submits no transfer/payment/trade; its only write is a reversible non-spending reminder/flag cell (MVP1-001 gate spend/trading prohibitions; LIFEOS-006 gate).
No autonomous legal commitment: the legal workflow sends/signs/files nothing; every commitment or external action is held for explicit human authority (MVP1-002 gate; LIFEOS-006 gate).
Information is separated from advice and from action in the legal workflow, and each is a distinct, labeled artifact (MVP1-002 gate).

Source documents are preserved with provenance; no financial or legal claim exists in system state without a SHAKE256 witness rooted in the source fixture (MVP1-002 gate; blueprint Cryptographic Witness Chains).
Every reversible cell is COW-discardable and leaves original source bytes and external state unchanged on rollback (MVP1-001 reversible cells; blueprint COW branching).
Only proven state is promoted forward into canonical PostgreSQL learning; simulated/unapproved branches are discarded (LIFEOS-005 'promotion of proven state only').

shared_substrate_spine.principle: The 'same LifeOS substrate' is one ordered set of governed-execution stages (LIFEOS-003) resting on the intelligence-substrate memory tiers (LIFEOS-005). BOTH workflows traverse the identical stage sequence G0..G10; the connected proof is that the substrate is workflow-agnostic — the financial and legal slices differ only in their bounded data and their execute/non-act branch, not in the spine or the store owners.

stages.name: Intent capture
stages.store_owner: redb (STORE-001-B transient high-frequency) — the raw intent utterance and interaction trace land in the microsecond scratchpad first; only the settled intent record is flushed forward.

stages.name: Identity + Authority + Policy
stages.spine_source: LIFEOS-003 'through authority ... with no unlimited agent authority'
stages.store_owner: PostgreSQL + RuVector (durable relational macro-state: identities, permissions, policy) — the authority decision (scope, prohibitions, approval requirement) is a canonical row; secrets referenced, never stored raw (OS keyring, STORE-001 external-secret).

stages.name: Task graph / bounded plan
stages.store_owner: PostgreSQL + RuVector (durable relational macro-state; causal dependency edges) — the bounded step plan is a canonical, provenance-linked graph, not free agent latitude.

stages.name: Memory retrieval (hot/warm/cold)
stages.store_owner: hot=redb (recent traces/dedup), warm/cold=PostgreSQL + RuVector (canonical relational + semantic; hybrid ORDER BY semantic_embedding <-> retrieval); agent routing memory=AgentDB (.rvf) non-authoritative.

stages.name: Simulation (COW dry-run)
stages.store_owner: PostgreSQL COW staging branch (isolated from trunk) — recommendation and candidate cell are computed and dry-run in a cloned branch; simulation traces buffer in redb; nothing touches trunk yet.

stages.name: Human-approval gate
stages.store_owner: PostgreSQL (durable) — the explicit human decision (approve reversible cell / withhold commitment) is recorded as a canonical, audited row; no branch commits without it.

stages.name: Execution OR deliberate non-action (hermetic, reversible)
stages.spine_source: LIFEOS-003 'hermetic execution'; MVP1-001 reversible cells; MVP1-002 rollback-or-non-action
stages.store_owner: PostgreSQL trunk commit of the approved COW branch (reversible cell) OR a recorded deliberate-non-action decision; redb WAL shields the write during any PostgreSQL latency; forward-only.

stages.name: Proof
stages.spine_source: LIFEOS-003 'proof' on canonical PostgreSQL state; execution_cell=proof-cell
stages.store_owner: PostgreSQL + RuVector (durable) — an audit/proof row bound by a SHAKE256 witness chain to the source fixture and a causal edge; Causal Graph Verification rejects any rootless (bluffed) financial/legal claim.

stages.name: Rollback
stages.store_owner: PostgreSQL COW-branch discard + reversal of the reminder/flag cell using recorded receipts; original source bytes and external state unchanged.

stages.name: Next-action derivation
stages.store_owner: PostgreSQL (durable) — the verified next action is derived FROM the proof row (not invented) and recorded; routing/complexity metadata may live in AgentDB (.rvf) non-authoritatively.

stages.name: Measurable user value + proven-state promotion
stages.spine_source: LIFEOS-005 'proof-derived learning ... promotion of proven state only'; LIFEOS-006 'self-sustaining value'
stages.store_owner: PostgreSQL (durable) — the value metric and the promoted learning are committed ONLY because the proof passed; simulated/unapproved state is never promoted.

bounded_workflows.derives_from: MVP1-001 (governed financial operating slice) — read-only aggregation first, bounded recommendation, explicit approval, reversible execution, proof
bounded_workflows.slice_name: Recurring-charge shortfall awareness (read-only aggregation -> reversible reminder cell)
bounded_workflows.smallest_slice_rationale: The least financial surface that still forces the whole spine: aggregate read-only transactions, detect one upcoming recurring charge that would overdraw the available balance, recommend, gate on approval, and write ONE reversible non-spending reminder. It moves no money, so spend/trading prohibitions hold, yet every stage G0..G10 does real work.
bounded_workflows.intent_G0: User asks LifeOS: 'Am I about to get hit with anything I can't cover?'

real_input_with_sanitized_fixture.shape: A real-shaped bank transaction export (CSV: posted_date, description, amount, running_balance) plus a recurring-charge schedule; structurally identical to a genuine export.
real_input_with_sanitized_fixture.sanitization: No real account numbers, names, balances, or credentials. Account id is a validly-formatted synthetic token; amounts/dates/merchant names are fabricated. No secrets/** content. Read-only; the fixture is never written back.

bounded_workflows.authority_G1_G2: Scope = read-only aggregation over the sanitized statement; policy prohibits any transfer/payment/trade; the only permitted write is a reversible reminder/flag; approval required before that write. No unlimited authority — the agent proposes, the human authorizes.
bounded_workflows.simulation_G4: In a PostgreSQL COW branch, aggregate transactions, project available balance against the next recurring charge date, and compute the shortfall and a candidate reminder cell. Dry-run only; trunk untouched.
bounded_workflows.approval_G5: Human sees 'LifeOS suggests: your $<amount> <merchant> charge on <date> exceeds projected balance by $<gap>; set a reminder to move funds?' and explicitly approves or declines.
bounded_workflows.branch_G6: EXECUTION branch — on approval, commit the COW branch to trunk, writing ONE reversible reminder/flag cell to canonical PostgreSQL. No bank action; no money moves. The cell is fully reversible.
bounded_workflows.proof_G7: Proof row records: the shortfall was deterministically derived from the fixture, the reminder cell id, and a SHAKE256 witness binding the finding to the source rows. No bluffed number enters state.
bounded_workflows.rollback_G8: Delete the reminder/flag cell by receipt id (COW-discardable); the sanitized fixture bytes are unchanged; no external financial state ever existed to reverse.
bounded_workflows.next_action_G9: Verified next action derived from the proof: 'Await user execution of the funds move (outside LifeOS in MVP1); decompose an action-capable transfer-draft task only under a future action gate.' No autonomous escalation.

measurable_value_G10.value: An overdraft/NSF fee event is avoided by surfacing the shortfall with lead time.
measurable_value_G10.baseline: Without LifeOS the recurring charge posts against insufficient balance and incurs a fixture-defined NSF fee; the user learns after the fact.
measurable_value_G10.metric: dollars_of_fee_avoided (fixture NSF fee) AND lead_time_days = charge_date - reminder_creation_date (must be >= the fixture's configured minimum lead time).
measurable_value_G10.proof_method: Deterministic re-run over the fixture must flag the same shortfall and produce a reminder with lead_time_days >= threshold; value is checkable, not asserted.

bounded_workflows.derives_from: MVP1-002 (governed legal operating slice) — information/advice/action separated, source documents preserved, provenance recorded, explicit approval for commitments, rollback or non-action proven
bounded_workflows.slice_name: Contract auto-renewal notice-window surfacing (document intake -> deadline/obligation -> deliberate non-action)
bounded_workflows.smallest_slice_rationale: The least legal surface that still forces the whole spine and demonstrates the NON-ACTION branch: intake one contract, preserve it, extract the auto-renewal date and the notice-period obligation, separate information from advice from action, and HOLD the commitment (sending notice) for human authority.
bounded_workflows.intent_G0: User asks LifeOS: 'Is anything about to auto-renew on me?'

real_input_with_sanitized_fixture.shape: A real-shaped lease/service contract (term, effective_date, auto_renewal clause, notice_period_days, governing_law) as preserved source text; structurally identical to a genuine agreement.
real_input_with_sanitized_fixture.sanitization: Fabricated parties, addresses, and dates; no real PII, no signatures, no secrets/**. The source document is preserved read-only with a content hash for provenance; never altered.

bounded_workflows.authority_G1_G2: Scope = read-only understanding + deadline/obligation extraction; policy prohibits sending, signing, filing, or any external legal action; a reversible calendar reminder is permitted with approval; the legal commitment (non-renewal notice) is human-only.
bounded_workflows.simulation_G4: In a PostgreSQL COW branch, extract the auto-renewal date and notice deadline, and simulate BOTH outcomes (act: send notice; not-act: allow renewal) so the user sees consequences. Dry-run; trunk untouched.
bounded_workflows.approval_G5: Human sees three separated artifacts — INFORMATION ('auto-renews <date>; notice required <notice_period_days> days prior, i.e. by <deadline>'), ADVICE ('LifeOS suggests: send non-renewal notice by <deadline> if you intend to leave'), and ACTION (send the notice) — and the ACTION is presented as requiring explicit human authority.
bounded_workflows.branch_G6: DELIBERATE NON-ACTION branch — LifeOS sends/signs/files NOTHING. It commits only a reversible calendar reminder (if approved) and records a deliberate-non-action decision for the commitment itself. Non-action is the proven outcome.
bounded_workflows.proof_G7: Proof row records: the deadline was deterministically extracted from the preserved document, the information/advice/action separation, the reminder id (if any), the recorded non-action on the commitment, and a SHAKE256 witness binding the extracted deadline to the source clause. No invented clause enters state.
bounded_workflows.rollback_G8: Delete the calendar reminder by receipt id; the deliberate non-action leaves ZERO external legal state to reverse; the preserved source document bytes are unchanged.
bounded_workflows.next_action_G9: Verified next action derived from the proof: 'Hold; await explicit human decision to send or not send the notice. If the user elects to send, route to a human-authored action-capable legal task under an action gate.' No autonomous commitment.

measurable_value_G10.value: A silent auto-renewal / missed notice-window is avoided by surfacing the deadline before the window closes.
measurable_value_G10.baseline: Without LifeOS the notice window lapses unnoticed and the contract auto-renews.
measurable_value_G10.metric: lead_time_days = notice_deadline - surfaced_date (must be >= notice_period_days + a configured buffer) AND missed_deadlines = 0 over the fixture set.
measurable_value_G10.proof_method: Deterministic re-run over the fixture must extract the same deadline and surface it with lead_time_days >= (notice_period_days + buffer); value is checkable, not asserted.

gate_clause_coverage.real_inputs_with_sanitized_fixtures: Both workflows are driven by real-shaped, content-sanitized fixtures (mvp1-003-fin-statement.sanitized, mvp1-003-leg-contract.sanitized): genuine export/contract structure, fabricated identifiers, zero PII/credentials, read-only. Real input shape, safe content.
gate_clause_coverage.authority: Both bind an identity+policy authority contract at G1/G2 with explicit prohibitions (no spend/trade for FIN; no send/sign/file for LEG) and a mandatory human-approval gate at G5; no unlimited agent authority; secrets stay in the OS keyring.
gate_clause_coverage.simulation: Both run G4 in an isolated PostgreSQL COW branch (blueprint COW branching); simulation_required=TRUE is satisfied because no candidate write reaches trunk before it is dry-run and approved.
gate_clause_coverage.execution_or_deliberate_non_action: FIN exercises the EXECUTION branch (a reversible, non-spending reminder cell committed on approval); LEG exercises the DELIBERATE NON-ACTION branch (commitment withheld, nothing sent/signed/filed). Both branches of the clause are demonstrated across the connected proof.
gate_clause_coverage.proof: Both emit a proof row on canonical PostgreSQL state (proof-cell), each bound by a SHAKE256 witness chain to its source fixture with a causal edge; Causal Graph Verification rejects any rootless claim — no bluffed financial/legal fact persists.
gate_clause_coverage.rollback: Both are reversible: FIN's reminder cell and LEG's calendar reminder are COW-discardable by receipt id; LEG's deliberate non-action leaves zero external state; original fixture bytes are unchanged. The task-level rollback restores the archived acceptance plan with no external state changed.
gate_clause_coverage.next_action: Both derive a verified next action FROM the proof at G9 (FIN: await user-executed funds move, decompose transfer-draft only under a future action gate; LEG: hold for human decision, route to human-authored action task if elected). Derived, not invented.
gate_clause_coverage.measurable_user_value: Both carry a concrete, checkable value metric at G10 (FIN: dollars_of_fee_avoided + lead_time_days; LEG: lead_time_days + missed_deadlines=0), each provable by deterministic re-run over the fixture — self-sustaining value per LIFEOS-006.

work_items.surface: Financial bounded workflow (MVP1-003-FIN): read-only recurring-charge shortfall awareness -> reversible reminder cell
work_items.decision: Accept the smallest financial slice as read-only aggregation first, one reversible non-spending reminder as the execution branch, and dollars_of_fee_avoided + lead_time_days as the value metric; no transfer, payment, or trade is ever executed (MVP1-001 gate).

work_items.surface: Legal bounded workflow (MVP1-003-LEG): contract auto-renewal notice-window surfacing -> deliberate non-action
work_items.decision: Accept the smallest legal slice as document intake + deadline/obligation extraction with information/advice/action separated, the commitment held as deliberate non-action, and lead_time_days + missed_deadlines=0 as the value metric; nothing is sent, signed, or filed (MVP1-002 gate).

Both workflows MUST traverse the identical governed-execution spine (LIFEOS-003) on the intelligence substrate (LIFEOS-005); the connected proof is that the substrate is workflow-agnostic and each stage's store owner is fixed by STORE-001 (redb hot, PostgreSQL+RuVector canonical, AgentDB non-authoritative, keyring secrets).

Fixtures are real in shape and sanitized in content (no PII, no credentials, no secrets/**), read-only, and content-hashed for provenance; they are produced at the proof-cell execution gate, not by this definition task.

Every write is scoped by identity+policy with explicit prohibitions and a mandatory human-approval gate; no unlimited agent authority; no autonomous spend or legal commitment; secrets remain in the OS keyring (STORE-001 external-secret).

Both workflows dry-run in an isolated PostgreSQL COW staging branch before any trunk commit; the legal workflow additionally simulates both act and not-act outcomes; no candidate write reaches trunk unsimulated or unapproved.

The connected proof demonstrates BOTH clause branches: FIN commits a reversible non-spending cell (execution); LEG withholds the commitment (deliberate non-action). Reversibility and non-action are each proven.

Each workflow emits a proof row on canonical PostgreSQL state bound by a SHAKE256 witness chain to its source fixture with a causal edge; Causal Graph Verification rejects rootless claims so no bluffed financial/legal fact persists (blueprint anti-bluffing).

Reversible cells are COW-discardable by receipt id; deliberate non-action leaves zero external state; original fixture bytes are unchanged; the task-level rollback restores the archived MVP1 acceptance plan with no external state changed.

Each workflow derives a verified next action FROM its proof row (FIN: await user-executed funds move, defer transfer-draft to a future action gate; LEG: hold for human decision, route to a human-authored action task if elected); next actions are derived, never invented, and never autonomously escalated to action.

Each workflow carries a concrete, deterministically re-checkable value metric (FIN: dollars_of_fee_avoided + lead_time_days>=threshold; LEG: lead_time_days>=notice_period+buffer, missed_deadlines=0); only proven value/learning is promoted forward into canonical PostgreSQL (LIFEOS-005 promotion of proven state only).

store_001_alignment.single_durable_writer: PostgreSQL + RuVector owns every durable class the acceptance touches (authority rows, task graph, aggregated facts, proof rows, provenance, value metrics) exactly once; redb is buffer/WAL/dedup only.
store_001_alignment.forward_only_sync: Intent and traces enter redb (hot) and flow forward via envctl(embed) to PostgreSQL; no undirected redb<->PostgreSQL dual-write anywhere in either workflow.
store_001_alignment.no_sidecar: Semantic retrieval (G3 hybrid search) uses RuVector as a native PostgreSQL extension; no separate vector database.
store_001_alignment.non_authoritative_cognition: AgentDB (.rvf) may hold agent routing/skill state (RuvLTRA/SONA) but is non-authoritative for the financial/legal macro-state, which lives only in PostgreSQL.
store_001_alignment.secrets_isolated: Any bank/legal-portal credential stays in the OS keyring; PostgreSQL stores provenance references, never raw secrets; redb never holds secrets.
store_001_alignment.no_silent_reclassification: This acceptance exercises the intended tiered path against disposable COW branches; it reclassifies no existing durable class and performs no cutover (STORE-001 'no unverified cutover' guardrail).

rollback.plan_verbatim: Restore the archived MVP1 acceptance plan; no external state is changed.
ordered_steps: Restore generated/task_families/MVP1-003.json and proof_records/MVP1-003.proof.json from the archived acceptance plan.
Confirm no fixture, bank, legal-portal, PostgreSQL row, redb entry, or .rvf file was written by this definition task (product_execution_performed=false).
Confirm the FIN reversible reminder cell and LEG calendar reminder were never committed (they exist only in the execution-gate contract, not here), so there is nothing external to reverse.
invariants_preserved: No external financial or legal action was taken (blocked_paths honored).
No secrets/** were read or written.
Original fixture and source bytes are unchanged; only the two planning artifacts exist.

open_dependencies: MVP1-001 and MVP1-002 must fully bound their financial and legal slices before this acceptance is decomposed into executable child tasks (this task's next_action).

The governed execution spine (LIFEOS-003) and intelligence substrate (LIFEOS-005) phase plans are referenced by gate language here; their generated phase-plan artifacts are produced upstream and the live proof-cell run consumes them.

Live execution of both workflows (fixture creation, COW dry-run, approval, reversible-cell commit, proof rows) runs at the proof-cell execution gate; this definition supplies the acceptance criteria that execution must satisfy.
completion_gate: Both bounded workflows (MVP1-003-FIN, MVP1-003-LEG) traverse the identical G0..G10 substrate spine; every one of the eight gate clauses is covered for both workflows with concrete acceptance criteria; every store touch is owned exactly once under STORE-001; the execution branch (FIN reversible cell) and the deliberate-non-action branch (LEG withheld commitment) are both demonstrated; and MVP1-003.proof.json records zero financial or legal action and product_execution_performed=false.
